rules.statement: Every sensor worker declaration (`class: sensing` in `.intent/workers/*.yaml`) that declares `mandate.scope.artifact_type` MUST appear in each declared artifact_type's `supported_sensors` array, and every entry in an artifact_type's `supported_sensors` MUST be backed by a sensor worker declaration whose `mandate.scope.artifact_type` array contains the artifact_type's id. The two sets — authored (artifact_type `supported_sensors`) and introspected (sensor declarations) — MUST be equal.
rules.rationale: ADR-091 D4. The cross-validation invariant mirrors ADR-079 D9's `operational_capabilities_decorator_backing` pattern (YAML↔AST coherence). A sensor declaring an artifact_type whose worker_id is not listed in that artifact_type's `supported_sensors` array is a coherence failure: the registry's authored view does not match the worker fleet's introspected view. An entry in `supported_sensors` that no sensor declaration backs is a phantom (ADR-079 D9 class) — a registered sensor reference with no live sensor implementation. Together with `operational_capabilities_decorator_backing`, this rule completes the F-41 ↔ F-42 boundary invariant; the cognate `action_supported_by_declaration` (F-43 forward) will complete the triad. Ships at `reporting` per ADR-091 D5 Phase 1; promotes to `blocking` at Phase 7 once #570 sensing-class reclassification lands. Sensor declarations without `mandate.scope.artifact_type` are excluded from the introspected set during the Phase-1 transition window so the nine misclassified `class: sensing` workers do not surface as phantoms before reclassification.

scope.notes: Context-level check — substrate is the cross-product of all sensor declarations and all artifact_type declarations. Per-file dispatch is never engaged. Both glob patterns appear in scope so an edit on either side triggers re-evaluation.
